highlights.text: I wrote yesterday: “Bear case tomorrow: Generally 6972-69 has to fail.” After basing out here all night, we lost it this morning, and finally got the first proper flush of 2026 down to 6925 after which we ripped back to 6969-72 to close.
We buy dips when institutions do, and institutions buy them via Failed Breakdowns.
Failed Breakdowns occur when price goes elevator down flushes a low, traps shorts, then we go parabolic.
We set an enormous low at 6955 this morning (from which we rallied to 7k+) and the Failed Breakdown of this low is an obvious long entry.
We rallied to 7034 after CPI then went elevator down .

Instead, wait for 6986 to hold and recover 6993 to try long.” Yesterday late day ES went elevator down trapped below 6986 (there was a big shelf of lows there), recovered 6993, and we ripped to 7k+ into the close.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Largest Sell Of 2026 For SPX. Will Bulls Buy The Dip?
